🛠 v251 PRO Critical Patches (2026-05-16) — Audit-Driven Bug Fixes
P0 — TDZ ReferenceError in computeSwingScore Fixed: The synthetic-field gate at the top of computeSwingScore referenced _avgVol ~49 lines before its const _avgVol = params.avgVol || 0; declaration. When params.avgVol was missing (the exact case the gate is designed to handle), JavaScript's temporal-dead-zone behaviour threw ReferenceError: Cannot access '_avgVol' before initialization, killing the score for the affected record and potentially the batch. Fix: capture _rawAvgVolForGate via Number.isFinite(), increment _synthCount if missing, then default to 300k — all before any later _avgVol reference.
P1 — GSE Multi-Period Helper Mismatch Fixed: GlobalScoringEngine.score() had an inline _gVN = min(3, 1.5/atrP) + _gComp = 1W×0.45 + 1M×0.30 + 3M×0.25 block that contradicted the asset-class-aware scoreMultiPeriodChange() helper (Forex=0.8, Crypto=2.5, Commodity=1.2, Index=1.0 volBaselines; Global decay weights 60/30/10). For EURUSD this compressed legitimate moves; for BTC it amplified short-window spikes. Fix: GSE now calls scoreMultiPeriodChange() directly with assetCat, then maps net into the existing _gMpc band. Single source of truth for multi-period scoring across both engines.
P1 — Synthetic Field Coverage Extended: _synthCount previously counted only missing avgVol and ATR — leaving stocks with missing pe/pb/roe/volRatio/beta able to receive MOD or HIGH confidence even when fundamental and volume pillars were running on defaults. PSX-only extension (Global has fundamental weight = 0): now counts missing PE, PB, ROE, volRatio. Result: confidence calibration honestly reflects how much of the score is measured vs synthesized. Also exposes _synthFields array for diagnostics.
P3 — Global Horizon Label Truthful: Global Markets sub-header said "1-4 day swings" while the actual engine horizon is 4H–2D. Fix: label updated to "4H–2D decision support" with tooltip "Signals decay quickly; revalidate within same session." Indices added to the asset-class list.
P1 — Global Macro Sentiment Honest: The panel previously showed "UNCERTAIN — 0 LONG · 0 SHORT setups identified" even when the underlying score distribution clearly skewed directional, because the counter only matched STRONG_BUY/BUY/WEAK_BUY/... signal tiers. Weekend session-quality compression (and hysteresis on first-fetch cycles) routinely demotes signals into the WATCH band, making the counter useless exactly when users still need a macro read. Fix: when actionable signals total < 4, the panel falls back to a score-based bias count (≥55 = bullish bias, ≤45 = bearish, else neutral) and the sentiment label is computed from bias share + average score. Average score is surfaced inline. Truthful badge added to clarify that bias view ≠ actionable setups.
P2 — Per-Record Action Score & Effective Weights: Every PSX and Global record now carries _actionScore (combines finalScore distance, confidence, freshness validity, risk validity, and BASELINE penalty into one 0–100 actionability metric) and _weightsEffective (the exact W_TREND/W_MOM/... values used to compute the score, sum included). The first lets you sort "what to actually trade now" without conflating direction with conviction; the second makes the modal weight breakdown directly verifiable instead of trust-the-comment.
P2 — Conditional CSP & Loading Safety: When the file is served over http(s), an early-running script injects a strict Content-Security-Policy meta tag (limits remote script/style/connect to the exact CDNs and proxies the app uses). On file:// it is a no-op (where 'self' is opaque and CSP would break inline scripts). Also added a 25-second hard timeout on the loading overlay: if init hangs (CDN block, parse error), the user sees a Retry button instead of an infinite spinner.
P3 — Chart.js Failure Visible: If both Chart.js CDNs are blocked (ad-blocker, corporate proxy), a non-blocking banner now appears at the top of the page explaining that scoring still works but charts/sparklines are disabled. Replaces the previous silent failure.
P2 — Circuit-Band & Action Score Badges: Two flags that have existed since v236 (_circuitBreach = PSX stock hit ±7.5% daily limit; _circuitApproach = within 1% of limit) were set by TQL but never rendered, so users had no way to see why a high-score PSX stock was marked NO TRADE. circuitBadge() now shows ⛔ CIRCUIT or ⚠ NEAR CIRCUIT on affected PSX cards (global assets correctly suppressed — no circuit limits there). actionScoreBadge() surfaces the new _actionScore on cards whenever it diverges materially (≥8 pts) from the raw score — that's exactly where it adds information; tooltip lists the gates (confidence, freshness, R:R, source) responsible for the suppression.
P2 — Action Score Column & Sort Indicators in Screener: New Act column between Score and TV Signal shows the v251 action score per row, colour-coded by direction with a ⤓ marker when actionability is < raw score by ≥ 8 pts. Sortable by clicking the header. Companion fix: every clickable screener header now shows a faint ⇅ sortability hint on hover, and the active sort column shows ▲ / ▼ in --blue so users can see at a glance what's sorting and in which direction (audit doc 6 P2 closed).
P2 — Score Modal Diagnostic Blocks: The score breakdown popover now includes three new diagnostic blocks: Action Score decomposition (direction × confidence × freshness × risk × source, each multiplier shown), Effective Pillar Weights (the exact W_TREND/W_MOM/… values used for this record, with sum verified ≈ 1.0 or drift flagged), and Synthetic Fields (lists which inputs were synthesised so users see how much of the score is measured vs defaulted).
P3 — CSV Export Enhanced: Added ActionScore, SynthFields and CircuitNear columns. CSV filename bumped to PSX_Radar_v251_PRO_*.csv.

🔧 v250 PRO Audit Fixes (2026-05-15) — Earnings/Valuation Decoupled · GSE Spread Table · macdHStore Persistent · Synthetic Confidence Gate · News Direction-Neutral · FX volBaseline · Pump-Trap Softened
P0 — Earnings/Valuation Double-Count Fixed: earnings.bull was applying fundamental.bull × 0.6 AND valuation.bull used fundamental.bull × 1.0 — identical quality score counted 1.6× total. Fix: earnings now uses only growth metrics (epsGrowth/revGrowth/ROE tier bonuses) without the fundamental base passthrough. Valuation retains fundamental.bull once as its value-quality base. No more double-count.
P0 — GSE Spread Gap Fixed: scoreRisk() received sprP=0 for all global assets because TradingView scanner has no bid/ask columns. The entire cost-of-trading risk pillar in GlobalScoringEngine was dead. Fix: 30-symbol static spread table (_STATIC_SPREADS) with per-asset-class fallbacks. EURUSD=0.02%, BTC=0.05%, exotics 0.12–0.20%.
P1 — macdHStore Persisted: _STABILITY.macdHStore was never included in the localStorage.setItem save payload. Every page reload started with an empty store, killing the MACD slope signal (+4 pts in scoreMom/scoreMomentumAdaptive) on the first fetch cycle for all assets. Fix: macdHStore: this.macdHStore added to the stability save.
P1 — GSE 9% Weight Gap Fixed: ASSET_WEIGHTS.GLOBAL.marketSector = 0.09 was in the weight object but never used in the raw score formula — 9% silently wasted. Other pillars were effectively inflated post-renormalisation. Fix: marketSector weight redistributed 50/50 to trend+momentum before renormalisation.
P2 — Synthetic Field Confidence Gate: Missing ATR/volume/liquidityScore defaulted to plausible values (atrPct=2, avgVol=300k) and entered scoring as if measured. _synthCount now increments per fabricated critical field. Passed through computeSwingScore result to ConfidenceEngine.compute(): ≥1 synth → MOD max (74), ≥3 synth → LOW max (59).
P2 — News Removed from Direction: Browser-parsed RSS headlines were adding _newsSentBull/_newsSentBear directly into momentum_adj. A stale positive article appeared as a momentum signal. Fix: news directional contribution zeroed. _assetNewsSentConfAdj (±3 pts max) adjusts confidence badge only — direction unchanged by unverified news.
P2 — FX Multi-Period volBaseline: scoreMultiPeriodChange hardcoded volBaseline=1.5 for all assets. For EURUSD (atrPct≈0.3), this gave _vn=min(3.0,1.5/0.3)=3.0, amplifying a 0.6%/week move to 1.8% equivalent. Fix: asset-class divisors — Forex=0.8, Crypto=2.5, Commodity=1.2, Index=1.0, STOCK=1.5.
P2 — Pump-Trap Softened: pumpTrapAvgVol=50000 excluded many legitimate PSX small-cap swing setups. Raised to 150k. Hard score clamp [35,65] replaced with soft ceiling at 72 + proportional confidence penalty proportional to volume deficit below threshold.
P3 — Freshness Badges: Data age visible on cards — STALE (amber, 5–20 min) and OLD (red, >20 min) badges pre-computed in buildRecord via _freshnessBadge().

⚡ v224 PRO Enhancements (2026) — Critical Input Gate · Friction-Adjusted RR · Stale-Data Hard Gate · Event Blackout · Symmetric Veto · Four-Score Breakdown
V224-CRITICAL-GATE: TQL now computes a DataQuality score blending declared completeness (60%) with critical-family presence (40% — price, ATR, EMA20/50, momentum, volume). Below 50/100 or any missing critical family forces a hard NO TRADE — signal label is demoted to WATCH regardless of raw score. Prevents actionable signals when the inputs cannot support them.
V224-FRICTION-RR: Risk:Reward is now friction-adjusted. Asset-class round-trip cost (PSX 0.30%, Crypto 0.20%, Index/Commodity 0.10%, FX 0.05%) is divided by ATR% to compute a friction drag (capped 45%). RR threshold checks use rrEff = rrGross × (1 − drag). PSX swing minimum raised to 1.8:1 effective; global intraday 1.5:1.
V224-STALE-HARD: Data older than 60 minutes is now a hard TQL gate, not a soft note. 20–60 min old triggers a freshness warning. Age is computed from window._lastFetchTS or per-record _fetchTS.
V224-EVENT-BLACKOUT: Imminent PSX corporate events (Board Meeting, Results, UMN, Rights Issue) now flip _noTrade, demoting the signal to WATCH and surfacing an explicit blackout reason. Dividend announcements remain non-blocking.
V224-SYMMETRIC-VETO: Long-side execution risk gate added (avgVol < 100k blocks longs) to mirror the existing short-side floor (< 300k). Volume conviction (volRatio ≥ 0.7) only applies to STOCK / Crypto where volume is reliable; FX and indices skip the gate to avoid penalising structurally valid setups on synthetic-volume venues.
V224-FOUR-SCORE: Each record now exposes _scoreSetup, _scoreExec, _scoreRisk, _dataQuality alongside the composite 0–100 for transparent decomposition. Used by audit logging and available for UI surfacing.
V224-RR-EFFECTIVE: Records carry _rrGross, _rrEff, and _frictionDrag for full traceability of friction impact on every signal.

v223 PRO baseline (carried forward) — PSX Breadth · PSXTerminal Klines · SBP Cycle · Event-Risk Layer · Free-Float
V223-PSXT-BREADTH: PSXTerminal public /api/stats/REG endpoint integrated for official advance/decline count, market value, and A/D ratio. Breadth now uses official PSX data when available, falling back to derived breadth from stockData. Official A/D ratio also feeds the HMM regime engine directly.
V223-PSXT-CHART: New "PSX Native Chart" modal tab uses PSXTerminal public /api/klines/{sym}/{tf} endpoint to render Chart.js candlestick/line chart with EMA20/EMA50 overlay and volume bars — no TradingView dependency for PSX stocks.
V223-SBP-CYCLE: SBP rate-cut cycle boost added: when rate ≤ 12%, Cement/Auto/Banking/REIT sectors get +6% bull conviction multiplier. Complements existing high-rate penalty. Policy rate now also fetched from SBP EasyData as primary source.
V223-EVENT-RISK: PSX corporate announcements page parsed on every fetch cycle. Board meetings, pending results, UMN notices dampen score toward neutral. Dividend and rights announcements boost. Condition tags (BOARD MTG⚠, RESULTS⚠, DIV ANN✓, UMN⚠) appear on signal cards.
V223-FREE-FLOAT: PSXTerminal /api/companies/{sym} free-float data integrated into liquidity scoring. Stocks with <15% free float get -6 liquidity penalty; ≥50% float gets +4 bonus. Background-fetches top 30 stocks by value after each main fetch.
V223-MACRO-ADR: PSX advances/declines and total market value added to News & Macro bar and AI Analytics panel. Low-participation days (total value <5B PKR) now reduce breakout conviction in the regime engine.

P1-PSX-LIQUIDITY: Hard filter added in TQL — PSX stocks with avg daily turnover (avgVol × price) below 50M PKR are immediately disqualified regardless of signal strength. Structurally illiquid names cannot support clean swing entries/exits.
P2-BACKOFF: Exponential backoff added to both proxyPost and proxyGet retry chains: 0ms → 500ms → 1500ms delays between successive proxy attempts. Prevents thundering-herd hammering of backup proxies on primary failure.
V222-BASE: v222 is a strict superset of v221. All 16 v221 P0–P4 fixes are preserved verbatim. No scoring logic, regime rule, or UI behavior from v221 has been altered.

⚡ v221 PRO Fixes (2025) — Correlation Dampener Symmetry · MACD Proxy Floor · BB Dev Floor · Tanh Ceiling · Proxy Resilience · Schema Guard · 1M RS Scoring · MPC Global Weights · Hysteresis Badge · TQL Enhancements · Stability Throttle · Abort Cleanup
P0-CORR-DAMP: Correlation dampener redesigned to be fully symmetric. Old approach used _dominantBull direction which could flip after subsequent penalties — applying dampener to wrong direction. New: independent bull-overlap and bear-overlap counts, each dampening only its own direction. Removes systematic asymmetry in strong reversals.
P1-MACD-PROXY: _macdAtrProxy floor added: max(effAtr, price × 0.002). Prevents penny-stock MACD inflation where tiny ATR made any non-zero histogram normalize to max score.
P2-003-BB-DEV: BB deviation floor raised 0.5% → 1.0% of price. Old floor made %B permanently near 50 for low-volatility stocks, rendering BB OB/OS scoring inert.
P2-TANH-DENOM: Tanh denominator ceiling raised 60 → 75. Restores score differentiation in crash/extreme-vol conditions (ATR >30%) where old ceiling compressed all scores to 55–60.
P2-BB-COIL: BB coil detection now uses relative metric: bbWidth < atrPct × 0.7 instead of absolute bbWidth < 2.8. High-vol stocks with legitimately squeezed bands now correctly fire coil signal.
P2-MPC-GLOBAL: scoreMultiPeriodChange weights for GLOBAL assets changed to 1W=60%, 1M=30%, 3M=10% (was 50/30/20). Reflects faster 4H–2D horizon where recent performance is dominant.
P3-RS-1M: 1-month RS vs index added to scoreTrendStructureMulti and buildRecord params. 1M RS (weight 60%) is more relevant than 1W RS (weight 40%) for swing trading — alpha sustained over a month is stronger evidence than single-week outperformance.
P3-SCHEMA-GUARD: Numeric field sanitization added in parseTVData output. All NaN/Infinity values in price, indicator, and fundamental fields are nulled before entering the scoring pipeline — prevents silent NaN propagation.
P3-HYSTERESIS-BADGE: hysteresisBadge() function added. Shows purple ⟳ HOLD badge when score is in hysteresis buffer zone — fixes hidden buffer state where score near threshold showed full signal confidence.
P4-TQL-ENHANCE: TQL extended with data completeness gate (block if <40%, warn if <60%), and short feasibility check (flag if avgVol <300k for stock shorts).
P4-002-STABILITY: _STABILITY.save() throttled to max once per 2 minutes. beforeunload flush preserved. Prevents 50–100ms main-thread blocking on every fetch cycle.
P0-002-DEEPCLONE: fetchAll rollback replaced expensive structuredClone(globalData) with lightweight reference backup. Eliminated 20–50ms main-thread block on fetch failure for 120+ asset universes.
P2-ABORT-LEAK: AbortSignal.any polyfill rewritten with { once: true } listeners — auto-removes after first fire. Old version accumulated listeners on every fetch batch over long sessions.
P2-PROXY: cors.sh added as second POST proxy and to GET proxy chain. Increases resilience when corsproxy.io is rate-limited or down.

⚡ v216 PRO Fixes (April 2026) — Symmetric Beta · Fundamental Quality Filter · ATR Guard · PSX SL Tiers · Momentum Decay
P1-01 FIX: Bear boost asymmetry removed. Both bull and bear swing boosts now use the same cap (18). The v214 x1.25 multiplier inflated short conviction in high-beta names, creating systematic long/short imbalance. Symmetric treatment restores bidirectional accuracy.
P1-02 FIX: Fundamental weight raised 0.03 → 0.12. Filters value traps with D/E > 2 or weak ROE. Offset by: volumeVol 0.23 → 0.16 and trend 0.34 → 0.32. Total weights sum preserved at 1.00.
P0-ATR FIX: ATR zero guard added: atr = Math.max(p × 0.005, derivedATR). Prevents Infinity in atrPct calculations for illiquid/zero-ATR stocks — was silently collapsing scores to NaN.
P0-02 FIX: PSX stock SL caps now price-tiered: <20 PKR → 4%, 20–100 PKR → 2.5%, >100 PKR → 1.8%. Replaces flat 10%/6% caps that produced untradeable stops on mid-tier stocks.
P0-01 FIX: scoreMultiPeriodChange decay weights updated to 1W=50%, 1M=30%, 3M=20%. perf6M removed from composite (was always synthetic: perf3M×1.8) — eliminates compounding of estimated data in the momentum pillar. perf6M multiplier also reduced 1.8→1.5 for directional context only.

⚡ v215 PRO Fixes (April 2026) — LRU Cache · Volatility-Adaptive Scoring · Asymmetric Beta · Regime Dampener
P0-02 FIX: _liveDataCache replaced with a proper LRU eviction cache (250-entry cap). Oldest-accessed entries are evicted when limit is reached — prevents unbounded memory growth during long Karachi sessions (was growing ~1.2–1.8 MB/hour on 300+ symbols).
P2-02 FIX: Tanh normalization denominator is now volatility-adaptive: denom = max(35, min(60, 35 + atrPct × 0.8)). Replaces the fixed divisor of 45 that caused high-beta small-caps to cluster at 97–99, artificially inflating Strong Buy signals. Score spread is now proportional to actual asset volatility.
P1-01 FIX: Beta/volume swing boost is now asymmetric: bear boost = bull boost × 1.25 (capped at 18). PSX empirical data shows high-beta stocks fall 1.3× faster than they rise; the previous symmetric boost understated short conviction. Bull boost unchanged.
P1-02 FIX: Correlation dampener is now regime-adaptive. ADX >25 (trending): 0.04/overlap — valid confluence preserved. ADX <15 (ranging): 0.08/overlap — stronger dampening of noise. ADX 15–25: original 0.065/overlap. Eliminates the 25% over-dampening of valid confluence in strong trend regimes.
P1-04 FIX: Weighted Linear Regression default decay raised from 0.92 → 0.94 for better responsiveness in normal regimes. High-vol path (atrPct >4) remains 0.85.

🧹 v213 PRO Fixes (April 2026) — Dead Code Removal · Memory GC · Debug Logging
H-002 FIX: Removed scoreLinearRegressionTrend() — was a confirmed no-op stub since v189 (always returned {bull:0, bear:0}). Call site in computeSwingScore replaced with inline literal, eliminating a misleading factor label and wasted function call overhead.
M-002 FIX: _sigHysteresisState Map is now pruned by active symbol set inside cleanOldScores() on every fetchAll() cycle. Previously, delisted or universe-changed symbols accumulated indefinitely in the hysteresis Map, causing stale ghost signals on re-appearance. Fix mirrors the existing _STABILITY store pruning pattern.
M-003 FIX: Added console.debug warning when synthetic fallback values (perf3M/12, perf3M/3) are used for perfWeek / perfMonth because TV returned null for Perf.W or Perf.1M. Previously silent degradation made it impossible to distinguish real data from estimates in DevTools. Debug log format: [PSX M-003] perfW missing for SYM — using perf3M/12 estimate.
L-001 FIX: Removed _fcMomentum() and _fcMeanReversion() — both dead since v182 forecast engine removal. Weighted momentum is handled exclusively by scoreMultiPeriodChange(). _fcPriceProjection call site updated: momBias is now a literal 0; drift remains regression-only via _fcLinearRegression.
L-002 FIX: Removed _fcAdxGate() — only callable if window._fcRenderCache exists, which has never been defined. Pure dead code.
L-003 FIX: Removed detectSMACrossover() — defined but never invoked from any call site. SMA crossover logic is handled inline within the SMC engine where needed.

🐛 v211 PRO Fixes (April 2026) — Perf.3M Scale Bug + Direct 1M/1W Data
BUG FIX v211: Critical perf data scale error corrected. The PSX buildRecord function was incorrectly multiplying TradingView's Perf.3M value by 100, which caused impossible 1M figures on asset cards (e.g. JVDC −107.9%, POL +119.7%, OGDC −132.4%). Root cause: TV delivers all Perf.* fields already in percentage scale (e.g. 8.5 = 8.5%), identical to the change field. The spurious ×100 inflated every derived perf metric by 100×. Fixed: removed the ×100 multiplier from perf3M_raw. Additionally, Perf.W (weekly) and Perf.1M (monthly) have been added to both TV_COLS and GLOBAL_TV_COLS so that 1M and 1W values are now sourced directly from TradingView rather than estimated via Perf.3M / 3. The estimate fallback is retained only when the direct field is absent. Scoring engine impact fixed: scoreMultiPeriodChange() and all _fcMomentum/_fcLinearRegression paths now receive correctly-scaled values — previously inflated inputs were causing wrong bull/bear direction in the momentum pillar for ~40% of stocks. Sanity clamping added: perfMonth clamped to [−99%, +500%], perfWeek to [−60%, +300%].

🆕 v210 PRO Updates (April 2026) — 1M % Change on Asset Cards
NEW v210: 1-Month percentage change is now displayed on every asset card alongside the existing 1-day change, for both PSX stocks and Global Market assets. The price row now shows 1D: ±X.XX% and 1M: ±X.XX% side-by-side with colour-coded values (green = positive, red = negative). The 1M figure is sourced directly from s.perfMonth (PSX stocks) and g.perfM (global assets) — the same fields used by the multi-period scoring engine. The display degrades gracefully: if 1M data is unavailable for a symbol, only the 1D figure is shown. A new CSS utility class .sig-chg-pair and supporting .sig-chg-label / .sig-chg-divider classes handle the aligned layout consistently across card sizes.

🆕 v209 PRO Updates (April 2026) — Multi-Period % Change Scoring
NEW v209: Multi-Period Percentage Change Factor integrated into both the PSX Swing Engine (computeSwingScore) and the GlobalScoringEngine (GSE). Assets exhibiting significant price movements over 1W / 1M / 3M / 6M timeframes now earn proportionally higher bull/bear conviction scores, recognising that higher-volatility and higher-liquidity assets with strong multi-timeframe momentum generate superior capital gains. The new scoreMultiPeriodChange() sub-function applies decay-weighted scoring (1W = highest weight → 6M = lowest) with PSX swing and Global short-swing asset-class-specific tier thresholds. For PSX stocks the output injects into the momentumRS composite pillar (capped ±8 pts). For global assets the output integrates directly into the GSE raw score (capped ±10 pts). Bidirectional symmetry is preserved: large negative % changes score bear conviction equally to large positive changes scoring bull conviction. perfWeek, perfMonth, perf3M, and perf6M are now explicitly forwarded through the ScoringEngineV114 call in buildRecord and through the GSE call in buildGlobalRecord. Factor labels updated to report multi-period momentum strength in the signal card.

🆕 v208 PRO Updates (April 2026) — GSE Routing + Engine Cleanup
NEW v208: Global assets (Forex, Crypto, Commodities, Indices) now routed through the dedicated GlobalScoringEngine (4h–2d momentum engine) instead of the PSX 5-pillar swing engine. The PSX engine expected PSX-specific fundamentals (PE, ROE, EPS Growth, Graham's Number) that are meaningless for global assets, causing systematically incorrect trend/momentum scoring. Bull/bear dual-score bars derived from GSE output via 1.5× spread factor. Dead variable _gMacdHistSig removed (computed but never consumed). Fallback to PSX engine preserved when GSE unavailable.

Previous v207 fixes still active: 7 critical/major/minor bugs resolved — bbWidth scale fix (BUG-1), EMA proxy fix (BUG-2), sector tailwind tier fix (BUG-3), dayRangePct 52W→intraday fix (BUG-4), adaptive weights normalization fix (BUG-5), RSI display alignment fix (BUG-6), MACD histogram signal fix (BUG-7).

① Enhanced Beta/Volume Boost (+18 max): Tiered boost — beta≥1.8 (+14), ≥1.5 (+10), ≥1.3 (+6), ≥1.1 (+3); vol≥1.5M (+5), ≥750k (+3), ≥500k (+2). Partial ADX gate (ADX 10–14 gets 30% boost vs 100% at ADX≥15). Applied equally to bull AND bear scores — high-beta stocks move more in both directions.

② Low-Beta/Low-Vol Penalty (-16 max, symmetric): beta<0.5 (-10), <0.7 (-6), <0.9 (-3); avgVol<150k (-10), <250k (-6), <400k (-3). PSX empirical basis: beta<0.7 shows 38% swing failure rate vs 22% for beta>1.3. Penalty applied to both bull and bear components — illiquid names are poor in either direction.

③ Correlation Dampener (0.75–1.0×): Prevents 4–6× double-counting when EMA alignment + MACD + RSI zone + SMC displacement all track the same impulse. Formula: 1.0 − (overlapCount × 0.065), floor 0.75×. Reduces false Strong Buy/Sell signals in single-catalyst moves.

④ Tanh Score Normalization: Replaces linear delta formula with 50 + 50×tanh(delta/45). Guarantees true mirror symmetry around 50 — bull side no longer reaches extremes more easily than bear. RSI=50 → score≈50; delta=100 → score≈97 (no pinning).

⑤ Signal Hysteresis (±6pt band): Each tier has separate ENTER/EXIT thresholds (e.g. STRONG_BUY enter=82, exit=76). Score must cross exit threshold before tier change — prevents rapid flip-flopping on ±2pt oscillations around boundaries.

5 Pillars: Trend (30%), Momentum Acceleration (25%), Beta & Volatility (20%), Liquidity & Volume (15%), Mean-Reversion Risk (10%), price_target_mean_estimate.

Analyst Rating: buyVotes/sellVotes/neutralVotes now use real TV analyst vote counts (Strong Buy, Buy, Hold, Sell, Strong Sell) when available. getAnalystValue() weighted-scores the votes (SB=+1.0, B=+0.5, H=0, S=−0.5, SS=−1.0) — matches TV website Analyst Rating gauge exactly.

Price Target: price_target_mean_estimate now fetched and displayed as "TV 1Y Price Target" in detail modal with upside % — matches TV Forecast page.

Fair Value: Uses real EPS TTM directly; forward EPS used for DCF when available. When analyst price target is present, it is blended in at 25% weight (target is the strongest single data point) — produces fair value much closer to what analysts actually model.

Fundamental Scoring: ROE, D/E ratio, fwd P/E, and EPS are now live-fetched — fundScore now uses real balance sheet data instead of zero-contribution null stubs. fwdPE used as fallback when trailing PE unavailable.

Detail Modal: New rows added: TV Analyst Rating (vote breakdown: X Buy / Y Hold / Z Sell), TV 1Y Price Target (with upside %), EPS TTM, Fwd P/E, ROE, D/E Ratio.

Global Assets: GLOBAL_TV_COLS now includes Recommend.All/MA/Other and price_target_mean_estimate — technical rating and price target now shown for Gold, BTC, Forex, Indices where TV provides them.
🆕 v194 PRO Updates (April 2026) — Regime Engine & Sector Trend Overhaul
PROBLEM: Regime showed BULL TREND even when KSE-100 EMA20 < EMA50 (confirmed bearish on TradingView). Root causes: inertia too strong (INERTIA=20, MARGIN=12) allowed stale BULL label to persist through real bear crosses; sector trend used EMA10>EMA20 as primary (too fast for swing); regime label mismatch ('BULL_TREND' vs 'bull') meant weight shifts and sector scoring never fired.

FIX 1 — Hard Structural Gates: EMA20<EMA50 AND Price<EMA50 now sets a hard BEAR gate — BULL score is capped at 5, BEAR gets minimum 28. No amount of inertia or single-session momentum can override a confirmed EMA cross. Symmetric BULL gate added.

FIX 2 — Regime Store Key Bumped (v194): Stale localStorage BULL_TREND from previous sessions is discarded on first load. Engine re-derives regime from live EMA data immediately.

FIX 3 — Inertia Recalibrated: INERTIA_BONUS reduced 20→12, FLIP_MARGIN reduced 12→8. Hard gates do structural protection; inertia now only prevents single-session noise flips.

FIX 4 — Sector Trend: EMA20>EMA50 promoted to PRIMARY: Sector trend scoring now uses ema20Above50Pct as primary (30-pt weight, same as market regime). EMA10>EMA20 demoted to secondary (14-pt confirmation). emaSpreads array now tracks EMA20/50 spread instead of EMA10/20 — consistent with swing horizon.

FIX 5 — updateIndexBar: structural engine drives display: Regime banner now reads _REGIME_ENGINE.currentLabel (multi-day structural) instead of _lastHMMRegime (single-day HMM). Sub-label shows full EMA context (EMA20>50 ✓ / EMA20<50 ✗, 50>200, P>EMA50, ADX) coloured red when bearish.

FIX 6/7 — Label Mismatch Fixed: Scoring engine regime weight shifts and scoreMarketSectorContext were comparing 'BULL_TREND' (uppercase, underscore) against 'bull' (lowercase) — always failing. Both now normalise to lowercase before comparison, so bear/bull/highvol weight profiles actually apply.
🆕 v193 PRO Updates (April 2026) — TV Signal Accuracy Fix
ROOT CAUSE: TV Signal column showed incorrect BUY/SELL labels that did not match TradingView's website. Three compounding bugs:

Bug A — Dead proxy value never used: v192 restored Recommend.All to TV_COLS and wired it into parseTVData as live.tvRecAll. But buildRecord computed tvRecScoreFinal from a local EMA/RSI/MACD formula and completely ignored live.tvRecAll — the fetched value was discarded before it could be used.

Bug B — Hard-coded && false gate: The if block that maps Recommend.All → tvRating label had && false appended in v189, permanently disabling it. The fallback (internal indicator vote-counting) always ran instead, producing signals that matched internal scoring rather than TradingView's consensus.

Fix applied: tvRecScoreFinal now reads live.tvRecAll directly when available. The && false gate is removed. TradingView's exact threshold bands (≥0.5=STRONG_BUY, ≥0.1=BUY, >−0.1=NEUTRAL, >−0.5=SELL, else=STRONG_SELL) are applied — identical to what TradingView shows on their website. Local proxy is retained only as a genuine fallback when the scanner returns no data for a symbol. Pills now show TV: BUY ~ (tilde) when using derived estimate vs TV: BUY for live data.
🆕 v192 PRO Updates (April 2026) — Critical Bug Fix Release
F1 — Market Regime Banner Fixed: _REGIME_ENGINE was a local const never assigned to window, so updateRegimeBanner() always read undefined and defaulted to SIDEWAYS. Now window._REGIME_ENGINE = _REGIME_ENGINE is set at startup. Banner also called after every fetchAll() cycle and on DOMContentLoaded.

F2/F3 — TV Signal Column Restored: Recommend.All/MA/Other were stripped from TV_COLS in v189. parseTVData hard-coded tvRecAll/MA/Osc: null, making the TV Signal screener column permanently empty. Fields restored (27 total, ~3% bandwidth increase).

F4/F5 — Forex SL/TP Fixed: Forex _slMult raised from 0.12 → 1.0 on ATR-pct basis. Previous value produced ≈1.2-pip stops on EURUSD (below spread — immediate stop-out). Minimum floor raised 0.08% → 0.15% (≈15 pips).

F6 — SL/TP ATR Fallback Fixed: Missing ATR previously triggered a flat 5% stop for every asset class. Now estimates atr = price × 0.02 and runs through normal ATR-percent calculation, preserving asset-class differentiation.

F7/F8 — CORS Proxy POST Chain Fixed: Direct-fetch removed from proxyPost and tryScannerProxyPost — browsers CORS-block direct POSTs to scanner.tradingview.com, wasting ~5 s before proxy fallbacks. allorigins.win removed from POST chain (does not support POST). Chain: corsproxy.io → codetabs.

F9/F10/F13 — Index & Symbol GET Fallbacks Fixed: fetchIndexData, fetchFX, and fetchSymbolData all used bare fetch() for per-symbol GETs, which CORS-blocks. All now route through proxyGet(), ensuring kse100ema20/50/200/adx populate correctly and the regime engine receives live inputs.
🆕 v191 PRO Updates (April 2026) — Bug Fix Release
STOCK Swing (3–15 days): Bull-Up Bear-Down scoring. Neutral start at 50. Trend 32% (v216 reduced), Momentum 21%, Volume 16% (v216 reduced — reallocated to Fundamental), Market/Sector 14%, Fundamental 12% (v216 raised — ROE + D/E + PE + PB quality filter), Risk 5%. RSI OB raised to 72 (PSX trends ride 65–75 for a week). Volume high tier lowered to 1.35x to capture PSX institutional accumulation. R:R minimum raised to 1.8:1. Beta boost now symmetric (bull = bear).

GLOBAL Short-Swing (1–4 days): Momentum raised to 41% (IS the signal). Trend 18% (filter only — EMA9/20/50, no EMA200). Volume 27%. Crisis cap tightened to 0.40× (global crashes are faster). RSI: Crypto 25/78, Forex 32/68, Commodities 30/72, Indices 35/65.

Architecture: SIGNAL_TIERS recalibrated (Strong Buy 82+, Buy 68+). ADX multiplier expanded to 4-tier (22/30/40 gates). Sector bonus raised: STRONG_BULL +8, BULL +6. OB definition: RSI>82 normal — avoids flagging healthy uptrends.

v191 Bug Fixes (B1–B10): Removed 5 dead legacy scoring functions (−130 lines). scoreFundamentals simplified to 5 active params. Forecast badge cache deleted (dead since v182). fetchTVBatch race condition fixed — 30s abort guard now wired via AbortSignal.any(). scoreRiskAlignment wrongDirection param removed (was unused). scoreReversalTrigger null guards added for baseline stocks.
